textPlain: Weird, I posted this last week and it got a couple of views but I assumed just wasn’t popular enough and now it’s back?!Anyway I’ve made a few little updates since this, the main one being you can share fireplaces with friends and enemies (both static and animated).Thanks for checking it out!
You could make a version for Android that uses as much processor as possible to heat up the device, and especially Galaxy devices, to actually warm the users' hands.

Perfect timing, I was looking for something to keep my apps warm this winter since they're not fond of the iPod Socks I've been using (too itchy).For people looking for desktop or TV versions, I can also recommend:- https://store.steampowered.com/app/2678480/Pixel_Fireplace/- https://hammertail.itch.io/pixel-fireplace- https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=sy8GU_19V4E
Sorry, I don't have an iDevice, so I'll have to take comfort in keeping my terminal warm instead.https://opensource.com/article/18/12/linux-toy-aafire
Make it mine crypto to actually generate heat.
looks cool, are you planning an Android version? what was the biggest challenge in making this?

Reminds me of the wonderful thing:
https://hammertail.itch.io/pixel-fireplace
If you promise not to sue, I’ll get right on it

Hadn’t really thought about it tbh, maybe. Depends how fussy Android widgets and the watch stuff (which is the challenge. iOS widgets are a pain and doing any dev pairing with Xcode and a watch… lets just say I’m glad it’s all on fire…)
